title: Uragoga stenophylla
taxonomy: Uragoga stenophylla is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family consists of over 13,000 known species, making it one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. Uragoga stenophylla is native to tropical Africa, specifically in the regions of tropical West and Central Africa. Further classification of the genus Uragoga places it within the tribe Psychotrieae, which comprises plants that share similar characteristics with the coffee family. The specific classification of Uragoga stenophylla is still a topic of ongoing research and study.

intro: Uragoga stenophylla, also known as Kola stenophylla, is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to West Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve fatigue, and serve as a natural energy booster due to its caffeine content. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga stenophylla include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Uragoga stenophylla is prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, or used in traditional medicine as a powder or capsule, although more research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy.
